title: OEE & SMED Optimization
tagline: Unlock machine availability and slash setup times to maximize shopfloor throughput.
problem: Typical mid-size factories operate at 40-60% OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) while believing their lines are fully loaded, leaving crores in potential capacity unutilized.
challenge: Long changeovers (setup times), frequent minor stoppages, and undocumented speed losses create hidden bottlenecks that stall production schedules and balloon overheads.
approach: Conduct detailed time-and-motion studies of current changeover processes.
Apply SMED (Single-Minute Exchange of Die) principles to convert internal setup operations to external.
Standardize tools, pre-heat settings, and organize shopfloor workstations (5S).
Implement real-time OEE tracking visual dashboards for shopfloor operators.
Conduct root-cause analysis (RCAs) on top downtime drivers using Kaizen methodologies.
expectedImpactRange: ₹3.0 Cr – ₹8.0 Cr in recovered annual capacity

title: Workforce Optimization
tagline: Eliminate labor waste, balance assembly lines, and improve operator productivity.
problem: Unbalanced workflows and lack of standard operating procedures lead to 15-35% manpower waste on manufacturing floors.
challenge: High absenteeism, skill gaps, manual material handling delays, and poor line balancing force factories to employ excess contract labor to meet delivery deadlines.
approach: Map operations using MOST (Maynard Operation Sequence Technique) or stop-watch time study.
Perform line-balancing analysis to distribute work content evenly among operators.
Design cell layouts to reduce excessive walking and non-value-adding material handling.
Create visual Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and implement multi-skill matrix training.
Introduce daily standup routines (huddles) and hourly production board monitoring.
expectedImpactRange: ₹1.0 Cr – ₹3.0 Cr annual savings in labor and productivity losses

title: Capacity & Throughput Bottleneck
tagline: Identify and break shopfloor bottlenecks to expand output without new machinery capex.
problem: Factories invest in expensive new equipment when 20-30% additional capacity is hidden within their existing machines and processes.
challenge: Uncoordinated process steps, batch-size inefficiencies, and lack of visibility on the actual constraint result in late orders, high work-in-progress (WIP), and capital tied up.
approach: Model plant-wide material flows and identify the true physical bottleneck (Theory of Constraints).
Optimize scheduling around the constraint to maximize its runtime (Drum-Buffer-Rope).
Re-engineer raw material feed and batch sizes to eliminate constraint starvation.
Execute targeted TPM (Total Productive Maintenance) cycles on constraint equipment.
Establish active bottleneck buffer management systems.
expectedImpactRange: ₹2.0 Cr – ₹5.0 Cr recovered annual output capacity

title: Lead-Time & Flow Optimization
tagline: Compress order-to-dispatch lead times by 20-40% to delight customers and free up working capital.
problem: Excessive work-in-progress (WIP), messy shopfloor layouts, and long wait times delay client shipments and inflate inventory holding costs.
challenge: Disconnected departments, push-scheduling systems, and complex material pathways cause parts to sit idle for 90% of their total factory stay.
approach: Create end-to-end Value Stream Maps (VSM) for high-runner product families.
Transition from push scheduling to a customer-demand-pull system (Kanban).
Redesign shopfloor layout from functional departments to continuous flow cells.
Implement supermarket storage points and visual material signals.
Establish strict FIFO (First-In, First-Out) lanes and reduce manufacturing batch sizes.
expectedImpactRange: ₹1.5 Cr – ₹4.0 Cr in working capital unlocked

title: Integrated Plant Transformation
tagline: A complete operational and cultural overhaul to establish a world-class manufacturing facility.
problem: Fragmented improvement initiatives fizzle out because of a lack of integrated management systems, shopfloor discipline, and standardization.
challenge: Siloed departments, disconnected KPIs, lack of frontline ownership, and lack of sustainability mechanisms prevent continuous excellence.
approach: Run diagnostic shopfloor assessments and align senior leaders on target metrics.
Deploy 5S, Visual Management, and Daily Management Systems (DMS) plant-wide.
Train local teams in Lean Six Sigma problem-solving (Kaizen) for frontline ownership.
Establish cascading KPIs from the COO to the shopfloor operator level.
Design and install sustainable standard work and audit frameworks for plant managers.
